used: "Migraine brain" is real. The cognitive fog, memory gaps, and inability to find words — that's your brain in a neurological state, not you being dramatic.
3 weather triggers that are silently causing your migraines.
400mg of vitamin B2 daily was shown to cut migraine frequency nearly in half. Almost nobody talks about it. Almost every neurologist knows about it.
Aerobic exercise 3 times a week was found to be as effective as topiramate for migraine prevention. In a head-to-head trial. Almost nobody gets told this.
Altitude changes trigger migraines through the same mechanism as barometric pressure. Flying, mountain trips, even tall buildings — your brain notices.
Children can get migraines in their stomach — with no head pain at all. Abdominal migraine is widely missed because nobody connects the dots.
Chronic migraine isn't just a physical condition. The mental toll it takes is something most people never talk about.
Clinical studies confirm it: weather is one of the top 3 migraine triggers. We built an app around that.
CoQ10 at 300mg/day reduced migraine days by nearly 50% in a controlled trial. It's sold at every pharmacy. Almost no one recommends it.
Cold on your head AND heat on your neck at the same time works better than either alone. The mechanism is vasodilation vs constriction in complementary areas.
Doctors used to say the aura was "not real." Here's what brain scans actually show happening during a migraine aura.
Having to explain your migraine to someone who's never had one is its own kind of exhausting.
Histamine in fermented foods (kimchi, kombucha, aged wine) is a hidden trigger for some people. It's not an allergy — it's an enzyme deficiency that's easy to test for.
How barometric pressure affects your brain — explained in 60 seconds.
I tracked my migraines for 90 days alongside weather data. The pattern was undeniable.
I used to get 4 migraines a month around the same weather system. Turns out it wasn't a coincidence.
If you clench your jaw at night, you may be triggering migraines you think are random. The TMJ-migraine connection is real and massively underdiagnosed.
Migraine attacks increase your risk of stroke — but only in specific groups and specific conditions. This is something every migraine sufferer deserves to know and discuss with a doctor.
Migraine doesn't just take hours. It takes days. Most attacks have four stages — and the ones before and after the headache are just as brutal.
Migraine is a neurological condition, not a headache. Here's why that distinction matters for treatment.
Migraine is the most underdiagnosed neurological condition on the planet. Millions of people have it right now and have been told it's "just a headache."
Migraine makes some people temporarily see the world as smaller or larger than it really is. It's called Alice in Wonderland syndrome. It's a real neurological symptom.
Migraine sufferers lose an average of 4 work days a month. This is how to get them back.
Migraine sufferers who track weather patterns report fewer surprise attacks. Here's the data.
Most magnesium supplements do almost nothing for migraines. The form you take changes everything. Here's what to look for.
Most migraine apps track your symptoms AFTER the attack. We think that's completely backwards.
Most people with chronic migraine also have anxiety or depression. It's not because they're weak. They share the same neural pathways.
Neurologists have known about barometric pressure and migraines for decades. Your app should too.
Neurologists rate migraine as one of the top 10 most disabling conditions on earth. So why does everyone treat it like a headache?
Nobody sees the hours spent lying in a dark room. Nobody counts the birthdays you missed. We see it.
One in seven people has migraine. That's more than one person in every room you walk into.
POV: it's a thunderstorm and you're in bed with an ice pack watching everyone else enjoy the rain.
POV: the barometric pressure just dropped 8 millibars and your body felt it before the weather app did.
POV: you feel the pressure behind your left eye and immediately start calculating whether you can still make it to work.
POV: you wake up at 3am and you already know it's going to be a migraine day before you even open your eyes.
POV: you wake up the day after a migraine and feel weirdly euphoric. That's the postdrome. It's real.
POV: you're 20 minutes into what you thought was a good day and then the visual snow starts.
POV: you're at a wedding, the lights are bright, the music is loud, and you feel the prodrome starting.
People with migraine are some of the most resilient people alive. They just have to be.
Peppermint oil applied to the temples was shown to be as effective as paracetamol in reducing tension-related head pain. And it works in minutes.
Pressing firmly on the greater occipital nerve (base of the skull, slightly off-centre) can interrupt a migraine in progress for some people. It's the basis of nerve block injections.
Science has known about the weather-migraine link since the 1980s. Nobody told you because it's hard to monetize.
Sleeping in on weekends disrupts your circadian rhythm enough to trigger an attack. The fix isn't sleeping less — it's sleeping consistently.
Some people lose the ability to speak mid-sentence during a migraine aura. It's not a stroke. It's aphasia — and it's terrifying if you don't know it's coming.
The FDA approved a class of migraine drugs in 2018. Most GPs still don't prescribe them. Here's what to ask for.
The acupressure point between your thumb and index finger (LI-4) has been studied for migraine relief and shows measurable results in trials.
The anxiety before a high-pressure weather system is as real as the migraine itself. You're not imagining it.
The average migraine sufferer waits 7 years before getting a correct diagnosis. Seven years of being told to drink more water and take paracetamol.
The connection between sleep disruption and migraine onset is more specific than "bad sleep." Here's the exact pattern.
The emotional crash after a migraine — the low mood, the exhaustion, the grief — is physiological. It's the postdrome. And nobody explains it to you when you're diagnosed.
The prodrome phase starts up to 48 hours before the headache. Yawning, food cravings, mood shifts — your body is already signalling.
The reason migraines make you hate light might have nothing to do with your eyes. It's a specific photoreceptor — melanopsin — that connects directly to pain pathways.
The research on estrogen and migraines has existed for 30 years. Why are women still not being told this?
The thing that cut my migraine frequency in half wasn't a medication. It was knowing what was coming.
The worst part of a migraine isn't the pain. It's knowing it's coming and not being able to stop it.
There's a number that predicts your migraine risk tomorrow. Here's what it is.
There's a protein your body releases during a migraine attack. Scientists spent 30 years targeting it. Now it's the basis of the newest migraine drugs.
Vitamin D deficiency is significantly more common in chronic migraine sufferers. Correcting it doesn't just help your bones.
What if your phone could warn you 24 hours before a migraine hits?
Why do migraines hit harder when a storm is coming? The science is wild.
Women are 3x more likely to get migraines than men — almost entirely because of oestrogen. The drop just before menstruation is one of the most consistent triggers in all of migraine research.
You cancel plans, you cancel your life, and then you spend the rest of the day feeling guilty about it.
You don't have to smell something bad to trigger a migraine. Any strong smell — even ones you like — can activate the trigeminal nerve.
You're not weak. You're not dramatic. You have a neurological condition that the world still doesn't take seriously.
You've had to leave events early so many times that people have stopped inviting you. That one stays with you.
You've tried cutting caffeine, changing your diet, sleeping more, sleeping less. And it still comes back.
Your coworkers think you call in sick a lot. They don't know you've already cancelled three things this month.
Your skin hurts during a migraine. Not metaphorically — actually hurts to touch. It's called allodynia and it affects over 60% of migraine sufferers.
